note: Air-battle showcases: per famous Eastern-Front battle, the supporting air army's key formations (representative headline OOB, not exhaustive). build-units creates each as an air:true unit parented to the army and clusters it (derived) around the army's monthly position for the battle window, so selecting the army at that battle shows real named formations with their own aircraft. Designations/aircraft are curated from references; placement is approximate. Unit names dedupe the matching Wikidata scaffolds. Stalingrad (8th/16th VA) and Kursk-south (2nd VA) are authored as full curated files instead.

units.name: Jagdgeschwader 54 Grünherz
units.summary: The 'Green Hearts' — Luftflotte 1's fighter wing over the Leningrad front for almost the whole siege, converting from the Bf 109 to the Fw 190 in 1943. (Promoted from a Wikidata scaffold.)

units.name: Kampfgeschwader 1 Hindenburg
units.summary: A Ju 88 bomber wing of Luftflotte 1 supporting Heeresgruppe Nord around Leningrad. (Promoted from a Wikidata scaffold.)

units.name: Jagdgeschwader 51 Mölders
units.summary: Luftflotte 6's principal fighter wing over the northern Kursk pincer (9th Army from Orel), flying Bf 109 G and Fw 190 A.

units.name: Stukageschwader 1
units.summary: The northern-sector Ju 87 dive-bomber wing supporting Model's 9th Army.

units.name: Panzerjägerstaffeln (Hs 129)
units.summary: The cannon-armed Henschel Hs 129 tank-buster squadrons that knocked out Soviet armour during the Kursk offensive.

units.name: 3rd Fighter Aviation Corps
units.summary: Savitsky's RVGK fighter corps thrown into the Kuban to wrest air superiority from Luftflotte 4 in the largest air battle of the Eastern Front to that point (April–June 1943).

units.name: 216th Fighter Aviation Division (9th Guards from Apr 1943)
units.summary: The P-39 Airacobra division of the 4th Air Army — home of Aleksandr Pokryshkin's 16th Guards Regiment, where the 'altitude–speed–manoeuvre–fire' tactics were forged. Redesignated 9th Guards Fighter Aviation Division during the battle.

units.name: 230th Assault Aviation Division
units.summary: An Il-2 Shturmovik division hammering the Wehrmacht's 'Blue Line' defences and the Novorossiysk front.

units.name: 218th Night Bomber Aviation Division
units.summary: A Po-2 night-bomber division of the 4th Air Army — including the 46th Guards 'Night Witches', the all-women regiment that flew harassing raids over the Kuban.

units.name: 1st Guards Fighter Aviation Corps
units.summary: A Guards fighter corps of the 1st Air Army covering the 3rd Belorussian Front's breakthrough in Belorussia.

units.name: 3rd Assault Aviation Corps
units.summary: An Il-2 assault corps battering the retreating German columns during Bagration.

units.name: 2nd Guards Bomber Aviation Corps
units.summary: A Guards Pe-2 bomber corps of the 1st Air Army.

units.name: 275th Fighter Aviation Division
units.summary: A 13th Air Army fighter division covering the offensive that finally lifted the siege of Leningrad.

units.name: 277th Assault Aviation Division
units.summary: An Il-2 assault division supporting the Leningrad Front's breakout.

units.name: 6th Fighter Aviation Corps
units.summary: A fighter corps of Rudenko's 16th Air Army winning air supremacy over the Oder and Berlin.

units.name: 9th Assault Aviation Corps
units.summary: An Il-2 assault corps pounding the Seelow Heights and the Berlin defences.

units.name: 6th Bomber Aviation Corps
units.summary: A Pe-2 bomber corps of the 16th Air Army over Berlin.
